items.title: Cortney Nickerson shares what being a CNCF ambassador means to her
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>How do you know when you've found your community?

CNCF Ambassador Cortney Nickerson shares what being selected as an ambassador meant to her: the pride, the validation, and the sense of responsibility to keep showing up and help others get involved in the things that matter to them.

#CloudNative #CNCF #CNCFAmbassador</article>

items.title: Fabian Ponce from OpenAI breaks down why open source is essential for builders. #AI #Shorts
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>AI writes code fast, but what about the maintenance burden? 

Fabian Ponce from OpenAI breaks down why open source is still essential for builders.  

#OpenSource #AI #Shorts</article>

items.title: CNCF On-Demand: What Happens After AI Writes Code
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>The adoption of AI coding tools has been on a tremendous rise, but generating code is only one small part of the software engineering puzzle. In this webinar, we'll explore what happens after AI writes code. We'll cover the different layers of validation available to software engineers for testing that code, examine where the bottlenecks are in those layers, and introduce "remocal development" using open-source tools like Telepresence and mirrord to solve them, so that engineering teams can fully utilize the benefits of AI and not just generate code faster, but actually ship faster.</article>

items.title: Treating Kubernetes as a Linux Distro: APT-Style Packaging with FluxCD | Andrei Kvapil, CEO, AENIX
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io


Treating Kubernetes as a Linux Distro: APT-Style Packaging with FluxCD | Andrei Kvapil, CEO, AENIX

Kubernetes platforms face the same challenge Linux solved decades ago: how to let users install only what they need and build community-driven package ecosystems. In Cozystack, we built an APT-like package system on FluxCD and OCI artifacts. The operator introduces Package and PackageSource CRDs - analogous to dpkg and sources.list - with pluggable repositories, dependency resolution, and content-based versioning. We'll cover how we solved the chicken-and-egg problem (deploying CNI via Flux when Flux needs networking), evolving from HTTP hacks to a clean solution with flux-aio and FluxCD's source-watcher pattern using OCI artifacts directly. Takeaways: - Designing Kubernetes-native package management inspired by APT - Using FluxCD source-watcher and OCI artifacts in practice - Enabling pluggable repositories for Kubernetes platforms - Building extensible platforms where users can fork and contribute packages</article>

items.title: What If Every Cozystack Change Became a Commit? | Simon Koudijs, configbutler.ai
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

What If Every Cozystack Change Became a Commit? | Simon Koudijs - Founder and Engineer, configbutler.ai

Cozystack exposes platform services as Kubernetes API resources, e.g. Postgres, Redis, Bucket, Kubernetes. Users interact in the usual ways: kubectl, a GUI (Cozystack dashboard), or even feeding their AI agents through an MCP. But who is making all these changes? And why did they do it? Git seems far away. But it doesn't have to be. What if every platform change automatically appeared as clean YAML in Git? Fully automated. Just a silent layer that turns API activity into a durable, readable history of infrastructure intent. The immediate win is auditability: instead of digging through logs or dashboards, teams get versioned history they can inspect, diff, and share. Think of it as reverse GitOps: instead of Git driving the platform, the platform continuously writes its intent back to Git. In this talk, I'll explain why Cozystack is a natural fit for this model, show it live, and explore what platforms gain when Git stops being the interface — and starts being the memory.</article>

items.title: Building a Sustainable Edge PaaS for Education: The BeBy.cloud Journey with Cozystack | Robert Galik
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Building a Sustainable Edge PaaS for Education: The BeBy.cloud Journey with Cozystack | Robert Galik - Founder of BeBy.cloud

In this session, we explore the intersection of sustainability and inclusive education through beby.cloud, an ultra-low-energy Edge-Cloud platform. Based on real-world implementation at the Košická akadémia softvérového vývoja (KASV), we will discuss the transition from k3s setups to a full-stack Cozystack environment. The core of our success lies in using beby.cloud to lay the foundations of "pure Kubernetes" education. This approach allows students to master the essentials before diving into the specific cloud architectures used by our industry partners like Deutsche Telekom IT Solutions or Siemens Healthineers, Visma Labs or AT&T Key highlights of the talk: Breaking Barriers: How we take students from non-technical backgrounds and turn them into cloud-ready talents. Pure Kubernetes Foundations: Using beby.cloud as a transparent, low-energy sandbox to teach core concepts without public cloud complexity. From Zero to Junior: Real stories of students without deep prior IT knowledge successfully transitioning into the IT sector through hands-on edge computing. Cozystack on Edge: Why a full-stack private cloud is the ideal next step for regional high-tech education.</article>

items.title: Building public cloud service on Cozystack | Sergei Makarov - Technical product manager, Cloupard
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Building public cloud service on Cozystack | Sergei Makarov - Technical product manager, Cloupard

We built a cloud service called DevSpace on top of Cozystack. In this talk, I will explain how our solution is designed, how it works internally, and which problems it solves for platform users. I will walk through the main architecture decisions, the key Cozystack components we rely on, and the way we combine them into a developer-facing self-service environment.</article>

items.title: Building a Multi-Cloud Service Mesh from the Ground Up with Kilo | Lu Servén Marín - SRE, AuthZed
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Building a Multi-Cloud Service Mesh from the Ground Up with Kilo | Lu Servén Marín - SRE, AuthZed

In this technical dive deep, Lu demystifies the components of a service mesh. They explore Kubernetes networking from the bottom up to show what it takes to connect containers across nodes, geographic locations, and ultimately clusters using WireGuard and Kilo as the network fabric. With this foundational knowledge, Lu demonstrates how to incrementally build a service mesh on top of Kilo to allow containers in one cluster to natively access services in another: starting with a static mesh, adding service discovery, and ultimately automating service mirroring. Finally, the talk discusses some of the challenges, limitations, and alternatives to building service meshes on top of Kilo.</article>

items.title: The Hardest Part of Building Your Own Cloud: Identity | Radhika Patwari & Raushan Prabhakar
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Mumbai, India (18-19 June, 2026), Yokohama, Japan (29-30 July, 2026), and Shanghai, China (8-9 September, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

The Hardest Part of Building Your Own Cloud: Identity | Radhika Patwari - Software Engineer 2, Uber & Raushan Prabhakar - Senior Software Engineer, Asper.ai

When you use a hyperscaler, identity just works. But when you build your own cloud platform, Identity and Access Management (IAM) becomes the hardest problem to solve. In this talk, we explore why identity is central to multi-tenancy, security, and developer experience in sovereign cloud platforms. Using Cozystack as an example, we’ll look at how authentication, permissions, and service-to-service identity change when you move beyond hyperscaler environments. This session is designed for anyone curious about how real cloud platforms are built and why IAM is the most complex piece of the puzzle.</article>
